title: Browser Runtime Proof
purpose: Verify a browser-visible workflow by running the app, interacting with the UI, and recording direct or blocked evidence.
trigger: Run when a task claims user-visible web behavior or local browser regression coverage.
allowedActions: open the target URL in a browser harness
forbiddenActions: use production credentials without explicit approval
claim direct browser proof from static code inspection only
ignore visible UI overlap, blank screens, or broken assets
gates: target URL loads without fatal console errors

evidence.direct: Browser harness loaded and interacted with the target workflow.
evidence.proxy: Screenshot or log evidence from another run was inspected.
evidence.local: Static or unit-level frontend checks only.
evidence.blocked: App would not start, auth unavailable, fixture unavailable, or browser harness unavailable.

escalation: Ask the human before using live credentials or mutating production data.
Stop if the browser proof needs external auth that is not available.
Report visual/runtime failures directly instead of downgrading them to warnings.
